channel.title: Land of Enchantment Games - News
channel.description: Updates from Land of Enchantment Games

item.title: Now Who Is The Doomer
item.description: OK, DOOMER? At times I have been accused of dooming too hard on the sad state of tech. This piece aims to be a bit more optimistic. It's so important to me for me and for my friends, to get this issue (on LLMs) right and to communicate a stance with clarity and punch. There is so much **FOMO**, so m

item.title: Why I Care
item.description: I was challenged by my friend... who is a devops engineer for a large and well known home improvement retailer about why I am so strongly opposed to generative AI usage in code, when he said: > "You don't even work in IT anymore, why do you care so much" My normal replies, lately after exploring it

item.title: Happy New Year - Cool Stuff Ahead
item.description: Discord Our Discord is growing in to a tight-nit small group of like minded solo devs (for now), working on our craft, we have many private channels with learning tracks and personal progress trackers where we can sign in and get to work on our games. Currently I have created the following tracks: -

item.title: On Santa Claus
item.description: Answering the Question Plainly In 1897, an eight-year-old girl named Virginia O’Hanlon wrote to a New York newspaper asking whether Santa Claus was real. Some of her friends said no. Her father suggested she write to the paper and ask. Francis P. Church's reply did not beat around the bush, > **Ye

item.title: Let's be gamedevs
item.description: Devops is dead, let's be gamedevs. and... Let's make something beautiful. If the art of code is to be considered. If beauty goodness and truth matter. If integrity of software is anything... Is not the most human thing to do, in an **a**nti **i**ntelligence world, **to buck the system and make indie
